# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

ESM-Suite Status Page built on **Upptime** (v1.41.0) — a GitHub-powered uptime monitoring system. Monitors four ESM-Suite services (production and dev frontend/backend) and publishes a status page to GitHub Pages at `esm-status.weber-online.com`.

## Critical Rule

**Only edit `.upptimerc.yml`** — all other files are auto-generated by Upptime and will be overwritten. This includes:
- `.github/workflows/` (8 workflow files)
- `api/` (JSON status data)
- `graphs/` (PNG response time charts)
- `history/` (YAML status history)
- `README.md` (status summary)

## Configuration

All monitoring and site configuration lives in `.upptimerc.yml`:
- **Monitored sites**: URLs, expected status codes, request methods
- **Status page**: theme, logo, favicon, custom CSS/JS, i18n (German)
- **Branding**: WEBER logo, glassmorphism UI with dark/light mode toggle, Apple design system

## Monitored Services

| Service | URL |
|---------|-----|
| ESM Frontend | `https://esm.weber-online.com` |
| ESM Backend API | `https://esm.weber-online.com/api/healthz` |
| ESM Dev Frontend | `https://esm-dev.weber-online.com` |
| ESM Dev Backend API | `https://esm-dev.weber-online.com/api/healthz` |

## GitHub Actions Workflows

All auto-generated from `.upptimerc.yml` — do not edit directly:

- **uptime.yml** — every 5 min: checks endpoints, commits to `history/`
- **graphs.yml** — daily: generates PNG response time graphs
- **response-time.yml** — daily: calculates response time metrics
- **summary.yml** — daily: regenerates README
- **site.yml** — daily: builds and deploys static status page to GitHub Pages
- **updates.yml** — daily: checks for Upptime framework updates
- **setup.yml** — on `.upptimerc.yml` change: re-initializes status page
- **update-template.yml** — weekly/manual: updates workflow templates

## How It Works

1. GitHub Actions hits each URL every 5 minutes
2. Records HTTP status and response time in `history/*.yml`
3. Updates JSON API endpoints in `api/*/`
4. Daily jobs generate graphs, update README, and rebuild the static status page
5. Incidents auto-create GitHub Issues when a service goes down

## Adding/Removing a Monitored Site

Edit the `sites` array in `.upptimerc.yml`. Each entry needs `name`, `url`, and optionally `expectedStatusCodes`, `method`, `headers`.

## No Build System

There is no `package.json`, no local build, no test suite. Upptime handles everything via GitHub Actions using the `upptime/uptime-monitor@v1.41.0` action.
